mcqs.question: What is the main difference between Propositional Logic and First Order Logic?

options.text: Propositional Logic is more expressive than First Order Logic
First Order Logic is more expressive than Propositional Logic
Propositional Logic and First Order Logic are equally expressive
Propositional Logic and First Order Logic are not comparable

mcqs.explanation: First Order Logic is more expressive than Propositional Logic because it allows us to represent knowledge about objects and their relationships.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of quantifiers in First Order Logic?

options.text: To assign properties to objects
To specify the scope of variables
To combine predicates and form complex expressions
To represent knowledge about propositions

mcqs.explanation: Quantifiers are used to specify the scope of variables in First Order Logic.

mcqs.question: What is the syntax of the First Order Logic expression ∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x))?

options.text: ∀x (Human(x) ∧ Mortal(x))
∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x))
∃x (Human(x) → Mortal(x))
∀x (Human(x) ∨ Mortal(x))

mcqs.explanation: The syntax of the First Order Logic expression ∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x)) is correct.

mcqs.question: What is the semantics of the First Order Logic expression ∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x))?

options.text: The expression is true if and only if all humans are mortal
The expression is true if and only if some humans are mortal
The expression is true if and only if all mortals are human
The expression is true if and only if some mortals are human

mcqs.explanation: The semantics of the First Order Logic expression ∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x)) define the meaning of the expression in terms of a model, which is a set of objects and their relationships.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the model in First Order Logic?

options.text: To assign properties to objects
To specify the scope of variables
To combine predicates and form complex expressions
To determine the truth value of First Order Logic expressions

mcqs.explanation: The model is used to determine the truth value of First Order Logic expressions.
